metadata.name: epoch_program
metadata.description: Dr Fraudsworth Epoch Program - VRF-driven tax regime coordination

instructions.name: consume_randomness
docs: Consume revealed VRF randomness and update taxes.
Called after Switchboard oracle has revealed randomness (~3 slots after trigger).
Verifies anti-reroll protection, reads VRF bytes, derives tax rates.
Client must bundle this with Switchboard SDK revealIx.
This is the third instruction in the VRF three-transaction flow:
1. TX 1: Client creates randomness account (separate transaction)
2. TX 2: Client bundles SDK commitIx + trigger_epoch_transition
3. TX 3: Client bundles SDK revealIx + consume_randomness (this instruction)
- `NoVrfPending` if no VRF request is pending
- `RandomnessAccountMismatch` if account doesn't match bound account (anti-reroll)
- `RandomnessParseError` if randomness account data is invalid

accounts.name: caller
docs: Caller (anyone can call after oracle reveals).

accounts.name: staking_authority
docs: Staking authority PDA - Epoch Program signs CPIs to Staking.

accounts.name: stake_pool
docs: Staking Program's pool state (mutable for update_cumulative).

accounts.name: carnage_state
docs: Carnage Fund state (for checking holdings and auto-expire).
Optional - if not provided, Carnage trigger check is skipped.
This allows backward compatibility and gradual rollout.

instructions.name: execute_carnage
docs: Called permissionlessly when atomic Carnage execution failed.
Must be called within 100 slots of the failure (carnage_deadline_slot).
This instruction performs the same execution as atomic Carnage:
1. If holdings exist and action = Burn: burn tokens, then buy target
2. If holdings exist and action = Sell: sell tokens to SOL, then buy target
3. If no holdings: just buy target token
All swaps are tax-exempt (0% tax, 1% LP fee only).
- `NoCarnagePending` if no Carnage execution is pending

accounts.name: epoch_state
docs: Global epoch state (has pending Carnage flags)

accounts.name: carnage_signer
docs: Carnage signer PDA - signs Tax::swap_exempt CPI

accounts.name: sol_vault
docs: Carnage SOL vault (holds native SOL as lamports)

accounts.name: carnage_wsol
docs: Carnage's WSOL token account (for swap_exempt user_token_a)
Box'd for stack savings (~165 bytes -> 8 bytes)

accounts.name: crime_vault
docs: Box'd for stack savings (~165 bytes -> 8 bytes)

accounts.name: fraud_vault
docs: Box'd for stack savings (~165 bytes -> 8 bytes)

accounts.name: mint_a
docs: WSOL mint (CPI passthrough, shared by both pools)

accounts.name: crime_mint
docs: CRIME token mint (mut: Token-2022 burn decrements supply)

accounts.name: fraud_mint
docs: FRAUD token mint (mut: Token-2022 burn decrements supply)

accounts.name: amm_program
docs: AMM Program (passed to Tax for swap)

accounts.name: swap_authority
docs: Tax Program's swap_authority PDA (signs AMM CPI within Tax::swap_exempt)

instructions.name: execute_carnage_atomic
docs: Called immediately after consume_randomness when Carnage is triggered.
Typically bundled in the same transaction for MEV protection.
This instruction executes the full Carnage flow:
1. If holdings exist and action = Burn: burn tokens, then buy target
2. If holdings exist and action = Sell: sell tokens to SOL via Tax::swap_exempt, then buy target
3. If no holdings: just buy target token via Tax::swap_exempt
All swaps are tax-exempt (0% tax, 1% LP fee only) via Tax::swap_exempt.
Swap amount capped at MAX_CARNAGE_SWAP_LAMPORTS (1000 SOL).
CRITICAL CPI DEPTH: This path reaches Solana's 4-level limit:
- `epoch_state`: Global epoch state (has pending Carnage flags)
- `carnage_wsol`: Carnage WSOL account for swap operations
- `InvalidCarnageTargetPool` if target pool doesn't match pending target

accounts.name: epoch_state
docs: Global epoch state (has pending Carnage flags)
NOTE: EpochState already has carnage_pending, carnage_action, carnage_target,
carnage_deadline_slot fields from Phase 23 - we READ these existing fields.

accounts.name: carnage_signer
docs: Carnage signer PDA - signs Tax::swap_exempt CPI

accounts.name: sol_vault
docs: Carnage SOL vault (holds native SOL as lamports)

accounts.name: carnage_wsol
docs: Carnage's WSOL token account (for swap_exempt user_token_a)
This receives wrapped SOL before swap and unwraps after
Box'd for stack savings (~165 bytes -> 8 bytes)

accounts.name: crime_vault
docs: Box'd for stack savings (~165 bytes -> 8 bytes)

accounts.name: fraud_vault
docs: Box'd for stack savings (~165 bytes -> 8 bytes)

accounts.name: mint_a
docs: WSOL mint (CPI passthrough, shared by both pools)

accounts.name: crime_mint
docs: CRIME token mint (mut: Token-2022 burn decrements supply)

accounts.name: fraud_mint
docs: FRAUD token mint (mut: Token-2022 burn decrements supply)

accounts.name: amm_program

accounts.name: swap_authority
docs: Tax Program's swap_authority PDA (signs AMM CPI within Tax::swap_exempt)

instructions.name: expire_carnage
docs: Called permissionlessly after the 100-slot deadline has passed.
Clears the pending Carnage state. SOL is retained in vault for
This instruction does NOT execute Carnage - it simply clears the
pending state so the protocol can continue. The accumulated SOL
remains in the Carnage vault and will be used on the next trigger.
- `carnage_state`: Carnage Fund state (read for vault balance)
- `sol_vault`: Carnage SOL vault (read for balance in event)
- `NoCarnagePending` if no Carnage execution is pending

accounts.name: epoch_state
docs: Global epoch state (has pending Carnage flags)

accounts.name: carnage_state
docs: Carnage Fund state (read for sol_vault balance in event)

accounts.name: sol_vault
docs: Carnage SOL vault (read for balance in event)

instructions.name: initialize_carnage_fund
docs: Called once at protocol deployment. Creates the Carnage Fund state
account and token vaults for CRIME and FRAUD.
The SOL vault is a SystemAccount PDA that will hold native lamports
from protocol fees. The token vaults are Token-2022 accounts that
will hold purchased tokens before burning.

accounts.name: sol_vault
docs: SOL vault PDA (SystemAccount holding native SOL).
Note: This is just a PDA address that will receive lamports.
We don't create an account here - SOL is stored as lamports in the PDA.

accounts.name: token_program
docs: Token-2022 program (for CRIME and FRAUD vaults)

accounts.name: program
docs: The Epoch program — used to look up its ProgramData address.

accounts.name: program_data
docs: ProgramData account — upgrade_authority must match authority.

instructions.name: initialize_epoch_state
docs: Called once at protocol deployment. Sets up genesis configuration
with CRIME as the cheap side, 3% low tax, 14% high tax.
None - all values are hardcoded for genesis.

accounts.name: program
docs: The Epoch program — used to look up its ProgramData address.

accounts.name: program_data
docs: ProgramData account — upgrade_authority must match payer.

instructions.name: retry_epoch_vrf
docs: Called permissionlessly if oracle fails to reveal within 300 slots (~2 min).
Replaces the stale randomness account with a fresh one.
Client must bundle this with Switchboard SDK commitIx.
This is a recovery mechanism to prevent protocol deadlock. If the original
oracle fails to reveal, anyone can call this instruction with a new
randomness account to restart the VRF process.
- `NoVrfPending` if no VRF request is pending
- `VrfTimeoutNotElapsed` if 300 slots haven't passed since original request
- `RandomnessParseError` if randomness account data is invalid

accounts.name: payer
docs: Payer for the retry (anyone can call).

accounts.name: randomness_account
docs: Fresh Switchboard randomness account (replaces stale one).

instructions.name: trigger_epoch_transition
docs: Called permissionlessly when epoch boundary is reached. Validates and
binds a Switchboard randomness account for anti-reroll protection.
Client must bundle this with Switchboard SDK commitIx.
This is the first instruction in the VRF three-transaction flow:
1. TX 1: Client creates randomness account (separate transaction)
2. TX 2: Client bundles SDK commitIx + trigger_epoch_transition
3. TX 3: Client bundles SDK revealIx + consume_randomness
- `payer`: Anyone, receives 0.001 SOL bounty from Carnage SOL vault
- `carnage_sol_vault`: Carnage SOL vault PDA (funds bounty via invoke_signed)
- `EpochBoundaryNotReached` if current slot hasn't passed next epoch boundary
- `VrfAlreadyPending` if a VRF request is already in progress
- `RandomnessParseError` if randomness account data is invalid
- `RandomnessExpired` if seed_slot is stale (> 1 slot behind)

accounts.name: payer
docs: Payer who triggers the transition. Receives the trigger bounty.
Anyone can call - permissionless epoch advancement.

accounts.name: carnage_sol_vault
docs: Carnage SOL vault PDA that funds the trigger bounty.
The vault accrues 24% of all trade tax and has ample balance for bounties.
Uses invoke_signed with PDA seeds to authorize the transfer.

accounts.name: randomness_account
docs: Created by the client in a prior transaction, passed here for validation.

errors.name: EpochBoundaryNotReached
errors.msg: Epoch boundary has not been reached yet

errors.name: RandomnessParseError
errors.msg: Randomness account data could not be parsed

errors.name: RandomnessExpired
errors.msg: Randomness account is stale (seed_slot too old)

errors.name: RandomnessAlreadyRevealed
errors.msg: Randomness has already been revealed (cannot commit)

errors.name: RandomnessAccountMismatch
errors.msg: Randomness account does not match committed account

errors.name: RandomnessNotRevealed
errors.msg: Randomness has not been revealed by oracle yet

errors.name: VrfTimeoutNotElapsed
errors.msg: VRF timeout has not elapsed (wait 300 slots)

errors.name: CarnageDeadlineNotExpired
errors.msg: Carnage deadline has not expired yet

errors.name: InsufficientTreasuryBalance
errors.msg: Insufficient SOL in treasury for bounty

errors.name: InvalidRandomnessOwner
errors.msg: Randomness account not owned by Switchboard program

errors.name: InvalidCarnageWsolOwner
errors.msg: Carnage WSOL account not owned by CarnageSigner PDA

errors.name: CarnageSlippageExceeded
errors.msg: Carnage swap slippage exceeded (below minimum output floor)

types.name: CarnageExecuted
docs: Contains full details of the buyback-and-burn (or sell) operation.

fields.name: sol_spent
docs: SOL spent on the swap (in lamports)

fields.name: sol_from_sale
docs: SOL received from sale (0 if action=Burn)

fields.name: atomic
docs: true if executed atomically in consume_randomness, false if via fallback

types.name: CarnageExpired
docs: Emitted when pending Carnage expires without execution.
Indicates that the fallback window (300 slots) elapsed without
anyone calling execute_pending_carnage. SOL is retained for next time.

fields.name: epoch
docs: Epoch when Carnage was originally triggered

fields.name: sol_retained
docs: SOL that remains in vault for next trigger

types.name: CarnageFailed
docs: Emitted when Carnage execution fails and funds carry forward.
Only emitted from expire_carnage when stale pending Carnage is cleared,
since failing transactions roll back entirely (no event emission).

fields.name: atomic
docs: Whether the atomic path was attempted (always true for expire, distinguishes from future use)

types.name: CarnageFundInitialized
docs: Emitted when Carnage Fund is initialized.
This event marks the initialization of the Carnage Fund vaults.

fields.name: crime_vault
docs: PDA of the CRIME token vault

fields.name: fraud_vault
docs: PDA of the FRAUD token vault

types.name: CarnageFundState
docs: This account tracks the Carnage Fund's holdings, vault addresses,
and lifetime statistics. The Carnage Fund accumulates SOL from protocol
fees and uses it for random buyback-and-burn operations.

fields.name: sol_vault
docs: PDA of the SOL vault (SystemAccount holding native SOL).

fields.name: crime_vault
docs: PDA of the CRIME token vault (Token-2022 account).

fields.name: fraud_vault
docs: PDA of the FRAUD token vault (Token-2022 account).

fields.name: held_token
docs: Use u8 to avoid Borsh enum serialization complexity.
See HeldToken enum for type-safe operations.

fields.name: held_amount
docs: Amount of held token (0 if held_token = None).
Represents tokens purchased during Carnage trigger,
waiting for next Carnage to burn or sell.

fields.name: last_trigger_epoch
docs: Used to track Carnage frequency for analytics.

fields.name: total_sol_spent
docs: Lifetime statistics: total SOL spent on buys (in lamports).

fields.name: initialized
docs: Set to true in initialize_carnage_fund, prevents re-initialization.

types.name: CarnageNotTriggered
docs: Emitted when Carnage trigger check occurs but doesn't trigger.
Indicates that VRF byte 5 did not meet the trigger threshold.

fields.name: vrf_byte
docs: VRF byte 5 value that didn't meet threshold (<11 required)

types.name: CarnagePending
docs: Emitted when atomic Carnage fails and enters pending state.
Indicates that Carnage was triggered but could not execute atomically
(e.g., due to compute limits). Fallback execution is now available.

types.name: EpochState
docs: This account is the coordination hub for all protocol dynamics:
- Tax rates (read by Tax Program during swaps)

fields.name: genesis_slot
docs: Used for epoch calculation: epoch = (current_slot - genesis_slot) / SLOTS_PER_EPOCH

fields.name: epoch_start_slot
docs: Slot when the current epoch started.

fields.name: cheap_side
docs: Cheap side gets low tax on buy, high tax on sell.

fields.name: low_tax_bps
docs: Low tax rate in basis points (100-400, i.e., 1-4%).
Applied to cheap side buys and expensive side sells.

fields.name: high_tax_bps
docs: High tax rate in basis points (1100-1400, i.e., 11-14%).
Applied to cheap side sells and expensive side buys.

fields.name: crime_buy_tax_bps
docs: CRIME buy tax rate in basis points.
If CRIME cheap: low_tax_bps. If FRAUD cheap: high_tax_bps.

fields.name: crime_sell_tax_bps
docs: CRIME sell tax rate in basis points.
If CRIME cheap: high_tax_bps. If FRAUD cheap: low_tax_bps.

fields.name: fraud_buy_tax_bps
docs: FRAUD buy tax rate in basis points.
If FRAUD cheap: low_tax_bps. If CRIME cheap: high_tax_bps.

fields.name: fraud_sell_tax_bps
docs: FRAUD sell tax rate in basis points.
If FRAUD cheap: high_tax_bps. If CRIME cheap: low_tax_bps.

fields.name: vrf_request_slot
docs: Slot when VRF randomness was committed (0 = none pending).
Used for timeout detection: if current_slot > vrf_request_slot + VRF_TIMEOUT_SLOTS, retry allowed.

fields.name: vrf_pending
docs: Whether a VRF request is pending (waiting for consume_randomness).

fields.name: taxes_confirmed
docs: Whether taxes have been confirmed for the current epoch.

fields.name: pending_randomness_account
docs: Pubkey of the Switchboard randomness account bound at commit time.
Anti-reroll protection: consume_randomness must use this exact account.

fields.name: carnage_pending
docs: Whether Carnage execution is pending (atomic failed, fallback active).

fields.name: carnage_target
docs: Target token for Carnage buy: 0 = CRIME, 1 = FRAUD.

fields.name: carnage_action
docs: Carnage action type: 0 = None, 1 = Burn, 2 = Sell.

fields.name: carnage_deadline_slot
docs: Slot deadline for fallback Carnage execution.

fields.name: carnage_lock_slot
docs: Slot until which only the atomic Carnage path can execute.
After lock expires, fallback execute_carnage becomes callable.
Set to current_slot + CARNAGE_LOCK_SLOTS when Carnage triggers.

fields.name: last_carnage_epoch
docs: Last epoch when Carnage was triggered.

fields.name: initialized
docs: Whether the epoch state has been initialized.
Set to true in initialize_epoch_state, prevents re-initialization.

types.name: EpochStateInitialized
docs: Emitted when epoch state is initialized at protocol deployment.
This event marks the genesis of the epoch system.

types.name: EpochTransitionTriggered
docs: Emitted when an epoch transition is triggered.
This event indicates that the VRF commitment phase has begun.
Client should bundle with Switchboard SDK commitIx.

fields.name: triggered_by
docs: Public key of the account that triggered the transition

fields.name: bounty_paid
docs: Bounty paid to triggerer in lamports

types.name: TaxesUpdated
docs: Emitted when taxes are updated after VRF randomness is consumed.
Contains the new tax configuration derived from VRF bytes.

fields.name: low_tax_bps
docs: Low tax rate in basis points (100-400)

fields.name: high_tax_bps
docs: High tax rate in basis points (1100-1400)

fields.name: flipped
docs: Whether the cheap side flipped from previous epoch

types.name: VrfRetryRequested
docs: Emitted when a VRF retry is requested after timeout.
Indicates the original VRF request timed out (300 slots) and a fresh

fields.name: original_request_slot
docs: Slot of the original (failed) VRF request

fields.name: requested_by
docs: Public key of the account that requested the retry
